evidence.description: Al-Sadr supported the 1979 Iranian Revolution and reportedly sent messages to Khomeini declaring him the leader of the Islamic world. His theory of wilayat al-faqih paralleled Khomeini's velayat-e faqih doctrine, and his ideas on Islamic governance influenced the constitutional architecture of the Islamic Republic.

editorial_notes: The alignment is intellectual and political — al-Sadr supported the Revolution and had parallel ideas on Islamic governance. The exact nature of their personal relationship is debated; marked alleged due to limited direct evidence of coordination.
